Bus Timings

Complete Guide to Visa on Arrival in Abu Dhabi: Requirements and Process

May 26, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Planning a trip to Abu Dhabi? Good news! Citizens of many countries can enjoy the convenience of getting a visa on arrival at Abu Dhabi’s Zayed International Airport. Whether you’re traveling for a short holiday or a business trip, the visa-on-arrival process is designed to be straightforward and hassle-free.

Who Is Eligible for a Visa on Arrival?

Citizens from a wide range of countries can obtain a visa on arrival in Abu Dhabi, free of charge. If you hold a passport from one of the following countries, you won’t need to apply for a visa in advance:

Eligible Countries for Visa on Arrival:

Andorra Hong Kong (SAR of China) Poland
Argentina Hungary Republic of Mauritius
Australia Iceland Republic of El Salvador
Austria Ireland Portugal
Bahamas Italy Romania
Bahrain Japan Russia
Barbados Kazakhstan Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
Belgium Kuwait San Marino
Brazil Latvia Saudi Arabia
Brunei Liechtenstein Serbia
Bulgaria Lithuania Seychelles
Canada Luxembourg Singapore
Chile Malaysia Slovakia
Costa Rica Maldives Slovenia
Croatia Malta Solomon
Cyprus Mexico South Korea
Czech Republic Monaco Spain
Denmark Montenegro Sweden
Estonia Nauru Switzerland
Finland Netherlands The Vatican
France New Zealand Ukraine
Germany Norway United Kingdom
Greece Oman United States of America
Honduras People’s Republic of China Uruguay

GCC citizens do not need a visa to enter the UAE.

Important Note: If your country is not listed, you will need to apply for a visa before your trip. This can be done through your nearest UAE embassy, a travel agency, or via Etihad Airways if you are flying with them.

Also See: Top 12 Free Places to Visit in Abu Dhabi for an Unforgettable Experience

Visa-on-Arrival Process: Step-by-Step Guide

For eligible travelers, obtaining a visa on arrival is a simple process. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you navigate through it easily:

  1. Arrive at Zayed International Airport, Abu Dhabi. After landing, follow the signs for immigration and visa processing.

  2. Approach the Visa Desk: There is no need to apply in advance if you are eligible for a visa on arrival. You will proceed directly to the visa desk upon arrival.

  3. Submit Your Passport: Hand over your passport to the immigration officer. No additional documentation is required for most travelers. In some cases, you may need to show proof of onward travel or accommodation, so it’s best to have these documents handy.

  4. Get Your Visa Stamp: The immigration officer will stamp your passport with a free entry visa. The visa duration is typically 30 days, though this may vary depending on your nationality.

  5. Enter Abu Dhabi: Once your visa is stamped, you’re free to explore Abu Dhabi! Enjoy your stay.

Pro Tip: Make sure your passport is valid for at least six months from your arrival date in the UAE.

What Documents Are Required?

For most visa-on-arrival travelers, you only need to provide:

Recommended Guides

Stay compliant and informed with our top administrative guides:

Step-by-Step Guide to Extending Entry Permits Common Emirates ID Fines and How to Avoid Them

  • A valid passport with at least six months of validity.

  • Proof of onward travel or accommodation (optional but recommended).

If you’re flying with a UAE airline or booking through a travel agency, they may also assist in confirming your visa status prior to travel.

How Long is the Visa Valid?

The typical visa-on-arrival is valid for 30 days, with the possibility of extension.

Can I Extend My Visa?

Read More: Ferrari World Abu Dhabi: Timetable, Attractions, and Must-See Rides

What If You’re Not Eligible for a Visa on Arrival?

If your country is not eligible for a visa on arrival, you will need to apply for a tourist or visit visa in advance. This can be done in several ways:

  • Through a Travel Agency: If you’ve booked a package, your travel agent can assist in applying for the visa.
  • At a UAE Embassy: Visit the nearest UAE embassy to apply for a visa. This is required for tourists, transit passengers, or long-term visitors.

Special Visa Options: Golden Visa

For individuals looking to stay long-term or invest in the UAE, the Golden Visa is an attractive option. The application process involves:

  • Applying for a nomination through the UAE government portal.

Once nominated, applicants can complete the process with the Federal Authority for Identity and Citizenship.

Getting a visa on arrival in Abu Dhabi is a smooth and efficient process for eligible travelers. By ensuring you have the necessary documents and understanding the process, you can look forward to a stress-free arrival. If you’re unsure about your visa status, it’s always best to check with the UAE government’s official website or contact your airline for assistance. Safe travels and enjoy your time in Abu Dhabi!

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: May 26, 2025.
